seo.title: Google ADK Guardrails for Government
seo.description: Compliance guide for Google ADK agents in government. Meet FedRAMP, FISMA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Government teams using Google ADK to build multi-agent systems with Gemini models face heightened compliance risks due to FedRAMP, FISMA, and NIST 800-53 requirements for AI in sensitive environments. Common ADK failure modes like multi-agent circular dependencies and session state corruption can lead to national security breaches or unexplainable decisions affecting citizens. Vex provides essential guardrails through before_agent_callback integration to ensure auditability, explainability, and regulatory adherence.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires continuous monitoring and auditing of cloud-based AI systems processing federal data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs all agent interactions and tool calls with tamper-proof audit trails, enabling FedRAMP-compliant monitoring.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk assessments for AI systems handling sensitive information with controls for access and data protection.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces data classification checks and blocks classified data from reaching external Gemini models.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires algorithmic impact assessments and ensures AI decisions are explainable and appealable.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex captures decision traces across multi-agent workflows, generating explainability reports for assessments.

complianceRequirements.description: Ensures AI outputs are accessible to individuals with disabilities for government services.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates agent responses for WCAG 2.1 compliance before delivery to citizens.

industryRisks.name: Classified Data Exposure
industryRisks.description: Multi-agent delegation accidentally routes classified information to external Gemini APIs.
industryRisks.example: Agent analyzing intelligence reports delegates PII extraction to unclassified tool, causing breach.

industryRisks.name: Unexplainable Citizen Decisions
industryRisks.description: Gemini model switching creates inconsistent decision paths that cannot be audited.
industryRisks.example: Benefits eligibility agent switches models mid-conversation, denying appeal due to lost reasoning.

industryRisks.name: Circular Agent Dependencies
industryRisks.description: Agents delegate tasks in loops, corrupting session state and producing unreliable outputs.
industryRisks.example: Policy analysis agent endlessly delegates to research agent, crashing during congressional review.

industryRisks.name: Tool Format Mismatches
industryRisks.description: Agents expect different response schemas, causing cascading errors in government workflows.
industryRisks.example: Veterans services agent misparses healthcare eligibility tool response, delaying benefits.

implementation.title: Install Vex and Configure ADK Callback
implementation.description: Add Vex as before_agent_callback to monitor all ADK agent executions with government compliance policies.
implementation.code:
import vex

from google_adk import Agent, Runner
from vex_sdk import VexClient

# Initialize Vex with government compliance profile
vex_client = VexClient(api_key="gov-secret-key")

@vex_client.policy("gov-fedramp-audit")
def adk_before_agent_callback(ctx, agent: Agent, input_data):
    # Block classified data
    if 'SCI' in input_data.get('classification', ''):
        raise ValueError('Classified data prohibited')
    # Log for audit trail
    ctx.log_event('agent_start', {'agent': agent.name, 'input': input_data})
    return True

# Apply to ADK runner
runner = Runner(callbacks=[adk_before_agent_callback])

implementation.title: Prevent Circular Dependencies
implementation.description: Vex tracks agent delegation graphs to detect and block circular dependencies in multi-agent government workflows.

implementation.title: Ensure Explainable Decisions
implementation.description: Capture complete decision traces across Gemini model switches for appealable government decisions.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Google ADK in Government: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: The Department of Veterans Affairs deploys a Google ADK multi-agent system to streamline disability benefits processing. The workflow involves a triage agent routing claims to medical review, eligibility analysis, and approval agents powered by Gemini 1.5 Pro. During peak demand, circular dependencies emerge when the eligibility agent delegates back to triage for clarification, corrupting session state across 30-minute conversations. Tool responses from disparate healthcare APIs arrive in mismatched JSON schemas, causing 15% of claims to be incorrectly denied. Classified veteran health data from VA systems risks exposure when delegated to external Gemini APIs without classification checks.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: With Vex integrated as before_agent_callback, circular dependencies are blocked at 3 delegation levels, maintaining session integrity. Classified data is automatically redacted, ensuring FISMA compliance. Vex normalizes tool responses to ADK schemas and generates explainability reports for every decision, enabling appeals. Audit logs captured 100% of interactions for FedRAMP reporting, preventing congressional investigation. The system achieved 99.7% accuracy, restoring public trust and avoiding contract debarment.

cta.heading: Comply with Government Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex with Google ADK today to secure FedRAMP authorization and protect national interests.
